does: This addendum provides safety metrics and evaluation updates for two variants of the GPT-5.1 model: an Instant version optimized for speed and a Thinking version designed for extended reasoning. The work introduces new safety benchmarks specifically targeting mental health domains and measures potential for users to develop unhealthy emotional reliance on the AI system. It presents quantitative results across these new evaluation dimensions alongside existing safety metrics, offering an empirical assessment of risks and failure modes specific to these model variants.
connects: This work builds on the established practice of AI safety documentation and system cards pioneered in responsible AI deployment protocols. It extends prior work on evaluating large language models for harmful outputs, bias, and misuse by introducing specialized assessment frameworks for psychological and emotional harms. The mental health evaluation framework responds to growing evidence that conversational AI systems can influence user wellbeing, building on interdisciplinary research bridging NLP safety and mental health disciplines.
enables: These new evaluation methodologies for emotional reliance and mental health impacts can become standard benchmarks for assessing future conversational AI systems, particularly as models grow more capable at mimicking supportive relationships. The empirical findings create baselines for understanding how model scaling, architectural choices, and reasoning capabilities affect psychological safety. This work enables more informed deployment policies around access restrictions for vulnerable populations and design requirements to prevent problematic user dependencies on AI assistants.
